title: SENTINEL APEX — Exploit for CVE-2024-38819 exploit
id: 37589845-4c82-5ef2-8627-18d68791784b
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2024-38819 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-38819
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/18
modified: 2026/06/18
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2024-38819 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-18T09:13:21Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2024-38819", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2024-38819 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:6097259; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-18, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Microsoft Confirms Defender RoguePlanet 0-Day Exploit and Working to Release Patch
items.description:
Microsoft has officially acknowledged a critical zero-day vulnerability in Microsoft Defender, publicly dubbed RoguePlanet, and confirmed it is actively developing a security patch to address the flaw. Tracked as CVE-2026-50656, the vulnerability was formally published on June 16, 2026, by the Microsoft Security Response Center (MSRC) and carries a CVSS score of 7.8 (Important) under [] 
 The post Microsoft Confirms Defender RoguePlanet 0-Day Exploit and Working to Release
items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] THREAT-INTEL advisory. Risk score 8.5/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items.ioc_note: Actor-attribution campaign — IOC set derived from threat actor research, not automated feed extraction.

apex.ai_summary: [P1] Exploit - critical severity (Risk 9.0/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. Actor attribution: CDB-CVE-GEN. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: CRITICAL PRIORITY - Patch within 24-48 hours. Activate IR playbook. Notify SOC lead and CISO.

ioc_paywall.message: 1 IOC(s) at 7.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
items.description:
Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet.

The vulnerability has now been assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS score: 7.8), with the tech giant describing it as a privilege escalation flaw.

"Microsoft is aware of an elevation of privilege in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine in Microsoft Defender

title: SENTINEL APEX — Exploit for CVE-2026-49083 exploit
id: 27cc4a6d-6cba-5f8c-8818-be3c50b28767
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-49083 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-49083
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/18
modified: 2026/06/18
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-49083 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-18T09:13:21Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-49083", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-49083 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1014344; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-18, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Multer vulnerable to Denial of Service via deeply nested field names
items.description:
### Impact

Multer is vulnerable to a Denial of Service (DoS) via deeply nested field names in multipart form data. The `append-field` dependency parses bracket notation in field names (e.g., `a[b][c]`) with no limit on nesting depth, allowing an attacker to force allocation of deeply nested object structures that consume CPU and memory. A single HTTP request with a crafted multipart body is sufficient to exploit this.

### Patches

Users should upgrade to `2.2.0` and configure `limits.fieldNestingDepth` to the minimum depth their application requires.

### Workarounds

Set `limits.fields` to a reasonable value to reduce the number of fields an attacker can send per request. This does not fully mitigate the issue but limits the impact.
